---
title: "PT5 Rough Draft 4.7.26"
author: "Hayden and George"
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
output:
  html_document:
    df_print: paged
---

# Monarch Butterflies

```{r include=FALSE}
setwd("~/Documents/VIZ_SPRING/data_viz_project_spring")
library(tidyverse)
library(skimr)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(ggplot2)
library(lubridate)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(sf)
library(plotly)
library(leaflet)
library(mapview)
library(gganimate)
library(gifski)
library(paletteer)
library(ggthemes)

df = read_csv("Xerces Site Counts by County.csv", n_max = 387)

distance_data_raw = read_csv("distance_per_gram.csv")

# reorder distance_data
distance_data <- distance_data_raw %>%
    # First sort by val. This sort the dataframe but NOT the factor levels
    arrange(`km/gram`) %>%    
    mutate(Animal = factor (Animal, levels = Animal)) 

# lat and long info for each location
site_locations = read_csv("sites.csv", 
                          col_select = c("SiteID","Site Name",
                                         "Latitude","Longitude"))

# a little bit of wrangling...
df_long = pivot_longer(df, 
                       cols = !c('SITE ID','SITE NAME','COUNTY or REGION'),
                       names_to = 'time_period',
                       values_to = 'count') %>% 
          mutate(
                season = case_when(
                    str_starts(time_period, "ES") ~ "Early Season",
                    str_starts(time_period, "LS") ~ "Late Season",
                    TRUE ~ "Peak Season"),
                year = as.numeric(str_match(time_period, "[0-9]{4}")))

# join lat and longs to site IDs
df_long = left_join(df_long, site_locations, by=c("SITE ID" = "SiteID"))

# only observations at peak season
df_peak = df_long %>% filter(season == "Peak Season")
```

### Monarch Butterflies have one of the most incredible migrations of any animal species. 

The eastern North American Monarch Butterfly travels a staggering 4800 km from southern Canada to northern Mexico every year. This impressive distance makes the Monarch's migration one of the farthest animal migrations, adjusting for body weight. 

```{r echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
lollipop_chart1 <- filter(distance_data, Animal != 'Brown Planthopper') %>%
  ggplot(aes(y = Animal, x = `km/gram`)) +
  geom_segment(aes(x=0, xend=`km/gram`, y=Animal, yend=Animal), color = 'gray') +
  geom_point(aes(color = Type), alpha = 0.9) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c('Terrestrial' = '#4daf4a', 'Marine' = '#377eb8', 'Bird' = '#e41a1c', 'Insect' = '#ff7f00'))+
  # scale_size_continuous(range = c(1, 10)) +
  labs(title = "Notable Animal Migrations\nAdjusting for Body Weight", x = 'km/gram (log)', y = NULL) +
  theme_classic()
  
lollipop_chart1
```

### Unfortunately, the Western Monarch Butterfly population has declined significantly since 1997.

```{r echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# df_long = pivot_longer(df, 
#                        cols = !c('SITE ID','SITE NAME','COUNTY or REGION'),
#                        names_to = 'time_period',
#                        values_to = 'count')
# 
# df_long = df_long %>% mutate(
#     time_period = factor(time_period, levels = tail(names(df), -3))
#   )

# site_count_years = df_long %>% 
#   drop_na() %>%
#   group_by(time_period) %>%
#   summarize(total_counts = sum(count))

site_count_years_final = df_long %>% 
  drop_na() %>%
  filter(!startsWith(as.character(time_period), "LS") & 
         !startsWith(as.character(time_period), "ES")) %>%
  group_by(time_period) %>%
  summarize(total_counts = sum(count))

bar_chart2 <- ggplot(site_count_years_final, 
                     aes(x=time_period, y=total_counts)) +
    geom_col(fill = "Orange2") +
    labs(title = 'Western Monarch Butterfly Population Over Time', 
         x = 'Year', y = 'Count of Monarchs') +
    theme_classic() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = .5)
    )

bar_chart2
```

### As a result of the declining Monarch population, efforts to track their population has increased. 

In California, the number of Monarch tracking sites has steadily grown since 1997.

```{r echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
df2 <- read_csv("sites_monitored.csv", n_max = 43)
midseason_sites <- df2 %>%
  filter(Type == 'Mid-Season')

line_chart <- ggplot(midseason_sites, aes( x = Year, y = `Number of sites monitored`)) +
  geom_line(color = 'orange') +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = 'Monarch Butterfly Sites in California', y = 'Sites Monitored') +
  theme_classic()

line_chart

# Maybe this would be better as an infographic with little boxes for each site?
```

### Where are Monarch monitoring sites located? 

```{r echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
site_locations = drop_na(site_locations)

mapview(site_locations, xcol='Longitude', ycol='Latitude',
        crs=4269, grid = FALSE)
```

### At their observed peak in 1997, over 1.2 million Monarch butterflies were seen overwintering in California.

```{r echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(ggthemes)
df_1997 = df_peak %>% filter(year == 1997)

total_1997 = sum(df_1997$count, na.rm=TRUE)

CA = map_data('state', 'california')

p1 = ggplot(data=df_1997, aes(x=Longitude, y=Latitude)) +
    geom_polygon(data=CA, 
                 aes(x=long, y=lat, group=group),
                 fill='snow1') +
    geom_path(data=CA, 
                 aes(x=long, y=lat, group=group),
                 color='snow3') +
    coord_fixed(ratio=1.3) +
    geom_point(alpha=0.5, aes(size=count, color=count)) +
    scale_color_gradientn(
        colors=paletteer_c("ggthemes::Red-Gold", 30)) +
    # scale_color_viridis_c("plasma") +
    theme_void()

# p1 = p1 %>% 

ggplotly(p1)
```

### Since then, the number of butterflies observed overwintering at each site has decreased.

```{r echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# p = ggplot(data=df_peak, aes(x=Longitude, y=Latitude)) +
#     geom_polygon(data=CA,
#                  aes(x=long, y=lat, group=group),
#                  fill='snow1') +
#     geom_path(data=CA,
#                  aes(x=long, y=lat, group=group),
#                  color='snow3') +
#     # coord_map() +
#     coord_fixed(xlim=c(-125,-114), ylim=c(39,31),
#               ratio=1.3) +
#     geom_point(alpha=0.5, aes(size=count, color=count)) +
#     scale_size_continuous(name="Count") +
#     scale_color_gradientn(name="Count", colors=paletteer_c("ggthemes::Red-Gold", 30)) +
#     theme_void()  +
#     ggtitle("Year: {frame_time}") +
#     transition_time(year) +
#     ease_aes("linear") +
#     enter_fade() +
#     exit_fade()

# animate(p, nframes = 29, fps = 2, width = 400, height = 600,
#         # start_pause = 10, end_pause = 10,
#         renderer = gifski_renderer())

# anim_save(animation = p, "map2.gif")
```

![](map2.gif)
